1.gl: to ascend, intransitively (be high) or actively (mount); used in a great variety of senses, primary and secondary, literal and figurative

1.gl: properly, interrogative what? (including how? why? when?); but also exclamation, what! (including how!), or indefinitely what (including whatever, and even relatively, that which); often used with prefixes in various adv
1.kj: how (long, oft, (-soever)), (no-) thing, what (end, good, purpose, thing), whereby(-fore, -in, -to, -with), (for) why.
1.dv: or מַה; or מָ; or מַ; also מֶה; a primitive particle;

1.gl: a burden; specifically, tribute, or (abstractly) porterage; figuratively, an utterance, chiefly adoom, especially singing; mental, desire
1.kj: burden, carry away, prophecy, [idiom] they set, song, tribute.

1.gl: a gorge (from its lofty sides; hence, narrow, but not a gully or winter-torrent)
1.dv: or (shortened) גַּי; probably (by transmutation) from the same root as H1466 (גֵּוָה) (abbreviated);

1.gl: a roof; by analogy, the top of an altar
1.kj: roof (of the house), (house) top (of the house).

1.gl: strictly a demonstrative particle, here; but used of time, now or then

2.gl: to make a loud sound (like English 'hum'); by implication, to be in great commotion or tumult, to rage, war, moan, clamor
2.kj: clamorous, concourse, cry aloud, be disquieted, loud, mourn, be moved, make a noise, rage, roar, sound, be troubled, make in tumult, tumultuous, be in an uproar.

2.gl: to die (literally or figuratively); causatively, to kill

2.gl: drought; also a cutting instrument (from its destructive effect), as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement
2.kj: axe, dagger, knife, mattock, sword, tool.

2.gl: a battle (i.e. the engagement); generally, war (i.e. warfare)
2.dv: from H3898 (לָחַם) (in the sense of fighting);

3.gl: remote, literally or figuratively, of place or time; specifically, precious; often used adverbially (with preposition)
3.kj: (a-) far (abroad, off), long ago, of old, space, great while to come.

3.gl: to yoke or hitch; by analogy, to fasten in any sense, to join battle

3.gl: a bow, forshooting (hence, figuratively, strength) or the iris

3.gl: to bolt, i.e. figuratively, to flee suddenly
